meta.name: Harissa Yogurt Sauce
meta.subtitle: Creamy, spicy-tangy sauce for shawarma, kebabs, bowls, and roasted vegetables

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed as a shawarma-friendly sauce with adjustable heat.
Yogurt-based: keep cold, don’t leave out long.

summary: A creamy harissa-yogurt sauce with lemon, garlic, and a touch of honey (optional). Perfect for shawarma wraps, grilled meats, and roasted vegetables.

optional.label: Blender (optional)
optional.notes: Optional if you want an ultra-smooth sauce or to blend in herbs.

notes: Contains dairy (yogurt).
Harissa may contain traces of nuts or sesame depending on brand.

ingredients.label: Plain Greek yogurt
ingredients.notes: Use whole-milk Greek yogurt for richest texture; low-fat works but is thinner.

ingredients.label: Harissa paste
ingredients.notes: Start with 1 tbsp for mild; go up to 3 tbsp for hot (brand heat varies).

ingredients.label: Garlic, finely grated
ingredients.notes: Grate with microplane for a smooth sauce. Optional: use 1/2 clove for a gentler garlic note.

ingredients.label: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Adds gloss and rounds the heat.

ingredients.label: Honey (optional)
ingredients.notes: Balances harissa bitterness/heat. For vegan, use date syrup or omit.

ingredients.label: Cold water (optional, to thin)
ingredients.notes: Use only if you need a drizzle consistency.

ingredients.label: Fresh mint, finely chopped (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds a cooling lift; also works with parsley or cilantro.

steps.title: Mix the base
instructions: In a bowl, whisk the yogurt until smooth.
Add harissa paste, lemon juice, grated garlic, olive oil, and salt.
Whisk until fully combined and evenly colored.
checks: Sauce is smooth with no streaks of harissa or garlic clumps.

steps.title: Adjust heat, tang, and thickness
instructions: Taste and adjust: add more harissa for heat, more lemon for brightness, and a pinch more salt if needed.
If the sauce is too thick for your use, whisk in cold water 1 teaspoon at a time until pourable.
Optional: whisk in honey (or date syrup) to soften harsh heat/bitter notes; add cumin and black pepper if desired.
checks: Flavor is balanced: spicy + tangy + creamy; thickness matches intended use.

steps.title: Rest for best flavor
instructions: Cover and refrigerate for at least 15 minutes to let flavors meld.
Stir before serving. If thickened in the fridge, loosen with a splash of water or lemon juice.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Optional: fold in chopped mint (or other herbs) right before serving.
Serve as a drizzle or spread for shawarma wraps, bowls, grilled meats, or roasted vegetables.

notes: spicy
tangy
creamy
savory
Use thicker as a spread inside wraps; thin slightly for a drizzle over bowls.
Pairs well with pickled vegetables and fresh cucumbers for contrast.

fridge.notes: Store covered and refrigerated. Stir before use; add a splash of water or lemon if needed.

freezer.notes: Yogurt sauces can separate and become grainy after freezing.

notes: Keep refrigerated (dairy-based).
Do not leave out at room temperature for extended periods; discard if left out over 2 hours (or 1 hour in hot environments).
Use clean utensils to prevent contamination.

batchNotes: Double batch supports multiple shawarma meals for the week.
For meal prep, keep sauce in squeeze bottle for fast wraps/bowls.

notes: Harissa brands vary widely in salt and heat. Treat harissa amount as adjustable.
If using non-Greek yogurt, strain briefly or reduce added water.
If your harissa is very salty, reduce salt initially and season at the end.
If you want a brighter sauce, add a bit of lemon zest (optional, 1/2 tsp).
For a richer sauce, use labneh instead of yogurt (may need thinning).
Use 1 tbsp harissa and add 1 tbsp extra yogurt.
Add honey/date syrup to soften heat if needed.
Use 3 tbsp harissa and a pinch of cayenne if desired.
Balance with a touch more lemon and olive oil.
Fold in 2 tbsp chopped herbs (mint + parsley or cilantro).
Optionally add 1 tsp za'atar for a Levantine profile.
Omit garlic; add a pinch of cumin and extra lemon for lift.
Optional: add 1/4 tsp onion powder for savory depth.
